opath_nofollow_ok: open(O_PATH|O_NOFOLLOW) on a symlink under a bound volume names the link node. Fixed: on Linux the container-vfs O_SYMLINK emulation now carries O_NOFOLLOW alongside O_PATH so the bound/overlay open handle refers to the link, not its target.

excluded-macos: the page-straddle probes (get_mempolicy_straddle, straddle_*) place a fixed-size copyout object crossing into a PROT_NONE guard page; the macOS engine does not enforce PROT_NONE (mem.c forces host PROT_READ, cf. the mprotect case), so those reads never fault and return 0 instead of EFAULT. Linux engine still runs+passes the whole case.
